title: This morning's headlines
overview: An automated roundup of the most recent stories across the Brief's sources. Add an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret and tomorrow's çay comes with a written briefing.

sections.heading: Türkiye
sections.body: Turkey’s overseas credit card spending hit record in May (Turkish Minute) · Turkey's Ozel launches new party to challenge Erdogan (DW.com) · Iran says Turkey helped thwart planned offensive by Iraq-based Kurdish groups (Turkish Minute)

sections.heading: Around the region
sections.body: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist (BBC) · US to announce deal allowing Saudi Arabia a nuclear programme (BBC) · Trump: Iran will ‘pay a big price’ for US troop deaths (Al Jazeera)

sections.heading: Migration & residency
sections.body: Germany has deported a Uyghur man to China, his mother says (myMotherLode.com)

sections.heading: Safety
sections.body: Trump says US will destroy a bridge or power plant for each Iranian attack in the Strait of Hormuz (AP News) · Latest combat deaths show risk has shifted to troops far from Iran’s borders (The Washington Post) · Trump vows to hit an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship Iran attacks (Axios)

sections.heading: Money
sections.body: Turkey Rates to Stay Put as Middle East War Impedes Disinflation (Bloomberg.com)

closing: Full stories in the feed below.
